idea-plugin.name: VulnSpotter
idea-plugin.description:
A smart vulnerability scanner for your project dependencies. VulnSpotter integrates directly into your IDE to detect security vulnerabilities in your Gradle, Maven, npm, PyPI and Go dependencies. It leverages the OSV.dev database to provide real-time alerts, in-editor highlighting and detailed remediation advice without leaving your workflow.

Main Features

  • Automated Scanning: Detects vulnerabilities in Gradle, Maven, npm (package-lock.json), PyPI (requirements.txt) and Go (go.mod) projects automatically, and re-scans after Gradle/Maven sync.
  • In-editor Highlighting: Vulnerable dependencies are annotated directly in pom.xml and build.gradle with a one-click quick-fix that upgrades to the recommended fixed version.
  • Real-time Alerts: Get instant notifications about security risks in your dependencies.
  • Detailed Reports: View comprehensive vulnerability details, including summaries, CVSS severity, affected ranges and fixed versions, and export to HTML, PDF, CSV, JSON and SARIF.
  • Integrated Tool Window: A dedicated "VulnSpotter" tool window for managing and reviewing scan results, with direct/transitive classification and search by package or CVE.
  • Direct References: One-click access to official security advisories (CVE, GHSA, etc.).
  • Visual Indicators: Clear visual cues for safe vs. vulnerable packages.
  • Exclusions: Ignore specific dependencies or CVEs via a .vulnspotterignore file or the Settings page.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.0

  • Initial Release: First release of VulnSpotter on the JetBrains Marketplace.
  • Multi-ecosystem Scanning: Scans Gradle, Maven, npm, PyPI and Go dependencies for known vulnerabilities using the OSV.dev database, with automatic re-scan after Gradle and Maven sync.
  • In-editor Highlighting & Quick-fix: Vulnerable dependencies are annotated in pom.xml and build.gradle, with a one-click intention that upgrades to the recommended fixed version (and refuses to downgrade).
  • Tool Window: Dedicated tool window with vulnerability details, direct/transitive classification, search by package or CVE, and report export to HTML, PDF, CSV, JSON and SARIF.
  • Settings: Configurable minimum severity and ignored CVEs, persisted at the application level.
  • Layered Architecture: Codebase reorganized into a clean layered architecture.
  • Exclusions: Ignore specific dependencies via a .vulnspotterignore file.

action.description: Scan project dependencies for known vulnerabilities using OSV.dev
